item.title: Universe Broke
item.content:encoded:
This is great! I always love when an SCP can make me go "Wait a minute. Did it just…? Yes it did. Woah." It doesn't offer too much an explanation of a "working" universe which is nice, as it leaves it up to your imagination, which is the right thing to do here I believe.
+1.
+1.

Also nice to know all life is a mistake, not just me (And scene! End joke, post comment.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this SCP. + 1

alongside information regarding Incident 4634-02.

Addendum.2 | Discovery/Incident-4634-002:

The number of zeros is not the same.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I swear, this site can make any damn thing scary.

What's next, Chess?

Oh, and +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one was a rollercoaster, to say the least. My jaw was dropped for the entire second half of the page, and that last line hit me like a train.

+1 holy shit I wish I could give you +10

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dang, what a twist.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's explained through mention of DEEPWELL and the crosslink on 'exclusionary monitoring sites', but my answer here is basically as follows:

  1. You're correct when you say everything in our universe died when the universe was 'fixed' by removing the signs.
  2. The Foundation has at least one site and ome data storage that exist outside of the universe — in pocket dimensions and such. Places that are different 'rooms' on a cosmic scale.
  3. These places are not designed to repair a horrendously inimical universe, and they die, starve, break, or fizzle out as things outside (read: in the broken universe) get worse and worse.
  4. When things are broken (read: fixed) again by the mysterious entities responsible for the first lot of 4634, these spaces aren't changed. From the baseline Foundation's perspective, nothing happened except that the DEEPWELL database was full of documentation about this event, and ES-01 had recorded everything and then… moved on to whatever happens when you have a site existing closed off from the universe for centuries if not millenia. Probably either a Fallout vault-esque community or everybody dying1
  5. The time is indeterminate because DEEPWELL is designed to document changes between reality alterations, not to persist when the universe doesn't. At some point the universe is too broken to observe, and it shuts down. Likewise with Exclusionary Sites. At some point, people lost count, and given the nature of the reversion there's no way to externally verify anything.

Hope this cleared some things up!

Footnotes
1. My headcanon is the latter for verisimilitude's sake, but it's really up to you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I suggest you have another quick read through this to see if you get it but if you still want some (unofficial, not guaranteed correct) answers:

+ spoilers
- spoilers

[10:16] The destruction of all self-replicating compounds and the subsequent death of all life.

You're right: nothing did survive to witness this destruction. Everything was destroyed.

And then:

The resultant O0-Class Ontological Shift Scenario continued for an indeterminate amount of time, following which a further 128 instances of SCP-4634 manifested in an additional set of 128 monuments, and all changes reverted.

"all changes reverted" means every single change. Reality was restored to baseline after an unspecified amount of time where literally every molecule was in the "wrong" (subjective) place. It's impossible to measure how long this was because everything that could measure time was destroyed; but also because all evidence of time passing was reverted.

The big question: what is powerful enough to fix a universe?

EDIT: I should add that this is what makes this one of my favourite SCPs, excellent job on this MaliceAforethought

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just a few "little" nitpicks:

+ Show Spoilers
- Hide Spoilers 

how did humanity survive long enough to document all the changes that took place after the signs removed? If the rapid movement of the stars didn't destroy us, then the silicon re-arrangement should have. If that didn't kill us, then the carbon re-arrangement should have. There is absolutely no way that anything survived to witness the destruction of self-replicating compounds.

In addition to that, IF somehow SCP-2000 was activated by the aliens that broke the universe, how would we have found all the old documents? if everything containing carbon or silicon was destroyed, then all paper or electronic components would have also been destroyed, which would ruin any records of the universal restructuring.

And if breaking the universe DID fix everything, then the amount of time that life spent dead shouldn't be indeterminate. If the dead ages weren't so long that we didn't lose everything, then some electronic calendars should have survived, right? And if it was long enough that they didn't, then we could have used advanced archaeological and forensic techniques to determine the age of the old world structures.

Anyways, none of this really took away from the article too much. They're relatively minor plot holes, that are overshadowed by the quality of the article. Whether or not you fix them, I don't care. Without a doubt, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Initial thoughts: magical out-of-order signs, that's kind of interesting.

Later: Well that escalated quickly.

item.title: Re: Understandable have a great day
item.content:encoded: Very good on its own but add in the Lemon Demon ref in the discussion page? now it's golden

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is considered in the Foundation's best interests to ensure that the universe as a whole remains non-functional.

I mean, it's not something I thought I'd ever hear, but alright. Good read, interesting concept. Found it entretaining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fantastic. Easiest +1 I've given in a while.

For anyone who didn't get it
– hide block

Without the out-of-order signs placed on the doors leading outside of the monuments, the universe restructures to a state of pure order; crystalline grids of elements.
Thanks, strange alien intelligence, for breaking our universe in such a way that supports life <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-000014 was displaced 64 metres north as expected…

Aight cool.

…their skin and nervous system, however, were displaced 64 metres south

UH.

Anyway. Always been a stickler for fundamentally simple objects with big consequences/implications, and this is just super good. Big ol' +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The ratings module seemed to be non-functional when I viewed this, displaying only the current rating number, but not including the +/- buttons.

Edit: checked back, and it's working fine now. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This skip would certainly explain a lot of things in my life.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the concept that for life to exist, there cannot be a purpose for life. As if the reason everything is imperfect is because lift wouldn’t exist without imperfection. I love it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 4634 and 184 together remind me of the Keter Duty 001

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good stuff. I like the idea that a chaotic, broken universe is the only one in which human life can flourish. Your descriptions of the tests, and the cosmic "fixes" are very succinct and well written. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very, very good. Definitely thought of 184.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded: All valid nitpicks, which I have now fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-184 and CODE NAME: ████ ███ were big inspirations of mine while writing this; while I never intended for there to be any direct relationship between them, there's definitely thematic similarities, and I'm very glad that they came across :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is going into my head-canon as related to/containing SCP-184.

The universe being deliberately non-functional for whatever intended purpose it was made is also sadly believable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Each monument possessed door leading to an internal chamber containing a stone dais of unclear function,"

I think there's an "a" missing right by "door"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm.

So...
– hide block

The entire universe, which counts as a room, actually serves as something else that is totally opposed to human life?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy fuck that's such a fantastic ending line

Hard +1

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
For a Bright's Challenge article, the writing of this is pretty great. There are only a small number of nitpicks that I have:

+ Nitpicks
– hide block

affixed to a toilet at Site-55

I'm not entirely sure how I feel about using this site, as you mention they are stored at Site-90. This is definitely more of a personal thing though.

Culinary department test kitchen #7

You use the № symbol for Item Number, but you use # here. This is a little inconsistent, and I would probably choose one and stick with that.

Each monument possessed door leading to an internal chamber containing a stone dais of unclear function

This has already been mentioned, but you missed an 'a' before 'door'.

And that's about it nitpick-wise.

+ Overall (Spoilers?)
– hide block

I really like this. The idea of the universe being in an imperfect state is really cool, and it tackles the idea of what exactly a perfect state is. Also, just by this anomaly's existence, it poses some interesting questions:

What or who created this, and why?
Why is it here?
What is the intended function of the universe?

And I think I have some sort of idea for why. Your use of powers of two (64 metres, 128 characters, 256 instances) definitely comes across as intentional. And what uses powers of two? Video games and simulations.

So my thoughts about this is that it suggests we live in a simulation. And the entity running it implemented these for an unknown reason, maybe just to make it more interesting? I'm almost definitely wrong, it's just what came to mind.

But back to the actual quality. You've successfully justified the use of an esoteric class for me, and that's often not easy. I was interested all through the read, enough that I spent several minutes trying to think of answers to those questions. Well done.


Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to gee0765 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What in the hell?? It felt a little untoccused but it was thoroughly entertaining all through out and i loved that climax. Thank god there seems to be someone out there that broke it for us again

item.title: Understandable have a great day
item.content:encoded:
Symmetry's overrated, methinks
Look at the scars all over the Sphinx
I am lopsided, and so are you
Nothing worth loving isn't askew

So basically I was sitting in a toilet stall after throwing up and I noticed there was an out-of-order sign on the inside of the stall. I though "haha, wouldn't that mean everything outside the stall was out-of-order?" Then I spent the rest of the day in bed and wrote this.

Images are both license-compatible with sources linked in the captions.

This was written and coldposted for Bright's Challenge; it's my second successful submission, with the first being SCP-4177 - 50% Off.

Thanks for reading!
